title: 14 Uploading Files to ORS
body_text:
14	Uploading Files to ORS
14.1	Overview of Uploading Files to ORS
Financial institutions that use a banking system or an in-house system to create Canadian cheque return files (.RI), USD cheque return files (.RIU), AFT return files (.RTN), or AFT Notice of Change files (.NOC) can upload these files to ORS. Files can be uploaded to ORS in one of the following ways:
Manual upload to ORS via the ORS user interface
Uploaded to ORS via Central 1ГўВҖВҷs FTP server
Uploaded files
must
contain the correct file extension as follows:
.RI
for Canadian cheque return files
.RIU
for USD cheque return files
.RTN
for AFT return files
.NOC
for AFT Notice of Change files
Central 1 recommends using the following file naming convention:
File name XXXCCCMMDD.EXT where,
ГўВҲВ’
XXX
represents the 3 alpha characters that define the type of file (AFT = AFT return file, CHQ = cheque return file, or NOC = AFT Notice of Change file)
ГўВҲВ’
CCC
represents the 3 alpha characters that define the currency of the file (CAD or USD)
ГўВҲВ’
MMDD
represents the 2-digit month and the 2-digit day (e.g., June 14 = 0614)
ГўВҲВ’
EXT
represents the 3 alpha characters that define the file type (.RI ГўВҖВ“ cheque return file, .RTN ГўВҖВ“ AFT return file, and .NOC ГўВҖВ“ AFT Notice of Change file)
14.2	Uploading Files to ORS Manually
Proceed as follows to manually upload a cheque return file, an AFT return file, or an AFT Notice of Change file to ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Upload
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Upload Option
Select the file(s) you want to upload to ORS:
Cheque Return File
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Browse
beside the Cheque Return File field. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: File Upload
ГўВҲВ’
Locate the appropriate .RI or .RIU file, select it, and then select
Open
.
ГўВҲВ’
The name of the selected .RI or .RIU file shows on the File Upload page (along with the path indicating where the file is located). See Figure 3.
Figure 3: File Upload, Cheque Return File
AFT Return File
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Browse
beside the AFT Return File field. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: File Upload
ГўВҲВ’
Locate the appropriate .RTN file, select it, then select
Open
.
ГўВҲВ’
The name of the selected .RTN file shows on the File Upload page (along with the path indicating where the file is located). See Figure 5.
Figure 5: File Upload, AFT Return File
AFT Notice of Change File
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Browse
beside the Notice of Change File field. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: File Upload
ГўВҲВ’
Locate the appropriate .NOC file, select it, then select
Open
.
ГўВҲВ’
The name of the selected .NOC file shows on the File Upload page (along with the path indicating where the file is located). See Figure 7.
Figure 7: File Upload, AFT Notice of Change File
Select
Submit
to upload all selected files. The selected files, including the .RI, .RIU, .RTN, and/or .NOC files are displayed on the File Upload page. A status message is displayed on this page when the file upload is complete. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Status Message
Select the
cheque status
link to view the RI or RIU file details or select the
aft status
link to view the RTN or NOC file details. Once files have been uploaded, ORS will create three files ГўВҖВ“ .lst, .bak., and .err.
Select a
.lst
file to view the upload status and details of the file upload. The file status can be:
ГўВҲВ’
File Validated
ГўВҖВ“ The file was successfully uploaded to ORS with no errors.
ГўВҲВ’
File Rejected
ГўВҖВ“ The file was rejected because of a formatting error and no records were uploaded. An error message appears for each formatting error in the file.
ГўВҲВ’
Records Rejected
ГўВҖВ“ The file was accepted but there were errors with some or all of the records. An error message appears for each rejected record. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: AFT Upload Status
Select a
.bak
file to view an exact copy of the file uploaded to ORS. Select the link to inspect the file in case of errors.
Select an
.err
file to view errors. This file is created only if there are errors loading the file or errors in the records that correspond to the File Rejected and Records Rejected in the .lst file. The .err file doesnГўВҖВҷt contain information on what failed and is only used to show that there was an error. Specific errors are provided in the .lst file.
View the upload status details of the file (.lst). See Figure 10, Figure 11, and Figure 12.
Figure 10: Cheque Return (RI) Upload Status Details
Figure 11: AFT Return (RTN) Upload Status Details
Figure 12: Notice of Change (NOC) Upload Status Details
Use the browser to print the .lst file.
Related Information
Section 9.3
, Returning Cheques on ORS
Appendix #A-11
, ORS RI File Upload Error Messages
Appendix #A-12
, ORS RTN File Upload Error Messages
Appendix #A-13
, ORS NOC File Upload Error Messages
14.3	Uploading Files to ORS via Central 1ГўВҖВҷs FTP Server
AFT Returns files, NOC files, Canadian dollar cheque return files, or USD cheque return files can be uploaded to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs FTP Server. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on how to upload files to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs FTP Server.
Related Information
Section 1.3
, Contacts and Websites
Section 9.3
, Returning Cheques on ORS
END
